evals.prompt: I want to add SparkScan to my React Native app. Here's my screen file: EmptyApp.tsx. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete SparkScan integration code into EmptyApp.tsx as a function component with hooks, and shows the setup checklist (packages to install, npx pod-install, camera permission entries).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit-react-native-datacapture-core and scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode)
Setup checklist mentions npx pod-install for iOS
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription in ios Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions Android runtime camera permission (PermissionsAndroid) or notes Android auto-links
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API used)
DataCaptureContext.sharedInstance is used to obtain the context
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
License key placeholder (e.g. YOUR_LICENSE_KEY or similar) is present
new SparkScan(settings) is present (v8 constructor used, not SparkScan.forSettings)
SparkScan.forSettings( is NOT present
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies except EAN13UPCA and Code128 are enabled
<SparkScanView is rendered as JSX with context, sparkScan, and sparkScanViewSettings props
The component is written as a function component (no extends React.Component / class syntax)
useRef is used to hold the SparkScan mode reference across renders
useEffect cleanup calls dataCaptureContext.removeMode(...) on unmount
addListener is called on the SparkScan mode to register a listener with a didScan callback

Imports come from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core (DataCaptureContext) and scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode (SparkScan, SparkScanView, SparkScanSettings, SparkScanViewSettings, Symbology, SparkScanSession)

evals.prompt: Add SparkScan barcode scanning to ProductListApp.tsx. When a barcode is scanned, append it to the scannedProducts state and re-render the product list. Keep the existing clear button and list rendering.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds SparkScan to ProductListApp.tsx. The existing scannedProducts state, render logic, and clear button are preserved. Scanned barcodes are appended via setScannedProducts.

assertions.text: DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
new SparkScan(settings) is present
<SparkScanView is rendered in the component's JSX
sparkScan.addListener( is called with a didScan callback
didScan callback pushes / appends to scannedProducts via setScannedProducts (or equivalent state setter)
Existing scannedProducts state and setScannedProducts setter are preserved
Existing list rendering (FlatList / ScrollView / map) is preserved
Existing clear button / onClearTapped handler is preserved
useEffect cleanup calls dataCaptureContext.removeMode(...)

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my React Native app, I want to reject it if the data is '123456789' and show an error message 'Wrong barcode' for 30 seconds before resuming scanning. All other barcodes should show success feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a feedbackDelegate built with useMemo containing feedbackForBarcode that returns SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback for rejected barcodes and SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback for valid ones. The delegate is assigned on the SparkScanView via the ref callback.

assertions.text: The feedbackDelegate is assigned through the <SparkScanView> ref callback (view.feedbackDelegate = ...)
feedbackForBarcode is a method on the feedback delegate object
new SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback( is returned for rejected barcodes
resumeCapturingDelay of 30 is passed to the error feedback (in seconds)
new SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback() or SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback.fromVisualFeedbackColor is returned for valid barcodes
The rejection condition checks barcode.data === '123456789' or equivalent
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan mode, SparkScanView JSX, listener) is preserved
Imports for SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback and SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback are added
The feedback delegate is memoised (useMemo / useCallback / module-level constant) rather than recreated on every render

evals.prompt: I want to hide the standard SparkScan trigger button and instead use a custom React Native Pressable in my app's UI. When the custom button is pressed, scanning should start. When pressed again while scanning, it should pause.
evals.expected_output: The skill hides the standard trigger button (triggerButtonVisible = false), renders a custom Pressable/TouchableOpacity inside the SparkScanView children, and implements handlers that call sparkScanViewRef.current.startScanning() / pauseScanning().

assertions.text: view.triggerButtonVisible = false is set via the <SparkScanView> ref callback
A custom Pressable, TouchableOpacity, or Button is rendered inside the SparkScanView children
onPress handler calls sparkScanViewRef.current.startScanning() or equivalent
sparkScanViewRef.current.pauseScanning() is called in the pause branch
useRef<SparkScanView | null>(null) is used to hold the view reference
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan mode, SparkScanView JSX, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to change the SparkScan UI look for my React Native app. Hide the camera switch, hide the zoom switch, and set the trigger button tint color to white.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the appropriate visibility and color properties on the view via the ref callback, using v8 property names.

assertions.text: view.cameraSwitchButtonVisible = false is set via the ref callback
view.zoomSwitchControlVisible = false is set via the ref callback
view.triggerButtonTintColor is assigned a Color value (Color.fromHex('#FFFFFF') or equivalent)
Color is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan mode, SparkScanView JSX, listener) is preserved
Old v6 property names (captureButtonTintColor, torchButtonVisible) are NOT present

evals.prompt: I need custom behavior when the user taps the barcode count button in the SparkScan toolbar — I want to navigate to a summary screen using React Navigation. Wire this up.
evals.expected_output: The skill installs a uiListener via the ref callback on the SparkScanView and implements didTapBarcodeCountButton to trigger navigation.navigate(...).

assertions.text: view.uiListener is assigned an object via the <SparkScanView> ref callback
The uiListener object has a didTapBarcodeCountButton method
navigation.navigate(...) or equivalent router call is invoked inside the handler
useNavigation() or props.navigation is used to obtain the navigator
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan mode, SparkScanView JSX, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to clean up properly when my React Native scan screen unmounts — unbind the SparkScan mode from the context so its camera and native resources are released.
evals.expected_output: The skill wires dataCaptureContext.removeMode(sparkScanMode.current) into the useEffect cleanup function so it runs on unmount.

assertions.text: useEffect cleanup (returned function from useEffect) calls dataCaptureContext.removeMode(...)
The SparkScan mode passed to removeMode is the one held in the useRef
dataCaptureContext.dispose() is NOT called (singleton context must not be disposed)
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan mode, SparkScanView JSX, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to filter scans so duplicates within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my SparkScan settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter on the SparkScanSettings instance before constructing the SparkScan mode.

assertions.text: settings.codeDuplicateFilter = 500 is set (or equivalent in the API units)
codeDuplicateFilter is set BEFORE new SparkScan(settings) is called
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan mode, SparkScanView JSX, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I'm building a high-volume warehouse scanning app where we need rapid, continuous barcode scanning without pausing between items. My app already has SparkScan integrated: IntegratedApp.tsx. Reconfigure it with CONTINUOUS scanning behavior and PERSISTENT preview so the camera stays visible the whole time.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with defaultScanningMode set to new SparkScanScanningModeDefault(SparkScanScanningBehavior.Continuous, SparkScanPreviewBehavior.Persistent) and passes the settings via the sparkScanViewSettings prop on <SparkScanView>.

assertions.text: SparkScanViewSettings is created (new SparkScanViewSettings())
defaultScanningMode is set on the view settings
new SparkScanScanningModeDefault( is used with TWO arguments (scanning behavior AND preview behavior)
SparkScanScanningBehavior.Continuous is used (not Single)
SparkScanPreviewBehavior.Persistent is used (not Default)
The deprecated one-argument SparkScanScanningModeDefault(scanningBehavior) form is NOT used in code
The configured settings are passed via the sparkScanViewSettings prop on SparkScanView
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan mode, listener, component structure) is preserved
